claims.statement: The Worker Execution Interface accepts and emits only closed, schema-versioned, bounded canonical Work Specifications, Context Expansion Requests, Worker Reports, and Execution Records whose source bindings, sequence, capability facts, artifact references, and terminal facts are explicit; provider, model, session, transcript, source-body, and extension payloads are rejected.

One deterministic pure conformance Interface accepts the canonical lifecycle and fails closed on malformed, over-limit, duplicate, reordered, stale, cross-execution, capability-escalating, expansion-bypassing, contradictory, truncated-as-complete, or post-terminal inputs without spawning a worker.

Worker Reports and Execution Records remain observations only: they cannot create Evidence, approve Knowledge Closure, issue an Authority Decision, waive a Verification Obligation, satisfy a Claim, or move a Change to Accepted, while requested capabilities, declared controls, and observed enforcement remain distinct facts.

The fixed Worker Execution Interface proof accepts one canonical non-authoritative lifecycle and rejects lifecycle, digest-chain, replay, scope-escalation, expansion self-grant, provider-leakage, authority-injection, truncation, contradiction, and resource-exhaustion attacks.
